name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-70
title: test-rabbit-config-permissions.py t19 'confirmation strings reference settings.local.json' check is weakened by substring overlap
description: test/test-rabbit-config-permissions.py t19 (lines 270-277) asserts '.claude/settings.json' NOT in out_at — but the actual confirmation string is 'Added X to .claude/settings.local.json' which CONTAINS the substring '.claude/settings.local.json' which itself contains '.claude/settings.' (a fragment of '.claude/settings.json'). Since the test checks '.claude/settings.json' (with .json terminator), it correctly fails when the bare form appears, so the check works as-is. However the check could be tightened: assert ' to .claude/settings.local.json' explicitly to avoid future false-positive risk.
